title: Podochilus undulatus
taxonomy: Podochilus undulatus is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This plant is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia. Podochilus undulatus is further classified into the genus Podochilus, which is a small genus of orchids. Within the Orchidaceae family, Podochilus undulatus is a member of the subfamily Orchidoideae. Further classification is not well-documented for this specific species.

intro: Podochilus undulatus is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and wounds. Bioactive constituents of Podochilus undulatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
